buildingDept.name: Pinellas County Building & Development Review Services

fdep.name: Florida Department of Environmental Protection (FDEP)
fdep.note: Required for any structure over sovereign submerged lands. Small private single-family docks often qualify for exemptions.

acoe.name: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers (Jacksonville District)
acoe.note: Federal review for structures in navigable waters. Nationwide permits speed things up dramatically vs. Individual permits.

new-dock.label: New dock construction
notes: Small private single-family docks often qualify for an FDEP exemption but still need county sign-off.
Docks over 500 sq ft or in seagrass beds trigger longer FDEP + ACOE review.

dock-repair.label: Dock repair / rebuild in-kind
notes: In-kind repair (same footprint, same materials) usually only needs a county permit.
Any expansion of footprint bumps you back to full new-dock permitting.

boat-lift.label: Boat lift installation
notes: Lifts require ACOE and FDEP review because they occupy submerged land.
Many builders bundle lift permits with dock permits — ask if yours does.

seawall-new.label: New seawall or full replacement
notes: Replacement in the same footprint is generally faster than a new install.
Seagrass mitigation and endangered species review can add 8–16 weeks.

seawall-cap.label: Seawall cap-only refresh
notes: Cap-only work usually only needs a county building permit.
If the wall panels are structurally compromised, you'll be told to do a full replacement instead.

storm-rebuild.label: Post-storm rebuild (declared disaster)
notes: Post-disaster expedited review exists in Florida — declare intent to rebuild within 90 days for the fastest track.
FEMA 50% rule applies: if repair cost exceeds 50% of the structure's pre-storm value, current code applies (BFE, wind, elevation).
